⚠ API offline — showing last known data. Retrying in 30s
HOUR
FOREMAN EFF.
📊 Site Intelligence — Today: V2.4.2 Efficiency = Productive Active ÷ Detected Span · per-worker. Productive Active = Confirmed anchor dwell + Inferred active gap time (idle-corrected). Transit excluded from numerator. Ghost tokens (span ≥ 2h, zero productive movement) excluded from site average. NP reclassification: uncovered gaps site-wide during break windows 06:00–07:30 (shift entry/breakfast) and 12:00–13:00 (lunch/prayer) are reclassified as NP. Zone tags (Productive / Non-Productive) are server-side from anchor configuration — updated as site progresses. Most meaningful for the — workers with 2h+ productive time.

ℹ How is this calculated?
—
📐 Zone Efficiency — Methodology
Formula
V2.4.1: Efficiency = Productive Active ÷ Detected Span × 100
Productive Active = confirmed productive anchor dwell on floors 5+ (transit excluded). V2.3: FL0 (Lobby) = NP if dwell ≥10 min, transit overhead if <10 min — short pass-throughs (arrival/departure) not penalised. FL1–2 (Hoist/Welfare) always NP. Uncovered gaps during 06:00–07:30 & 12:00–13:00 break windows reclassified as NP site-wide. Detected Span = first → last anchor detection per worker.
Definitions
🟢 Productive Active — Confirmed anchor dwell in productive zones (excl. idle, transit, and NP-window gaps). Does NOT include inter-session uncovered time assumed productive in prior versions.
🟡 Transit Time — Gaps ≤30 min between anchor sessions (lift travel, floor changes). Classified as Non-Productive in V2.3 and excluded from efficiency numerator.
⏸ Idle Time — Reported by token firmware (Firmware v2). Cumulative per-worker daily counter. Only available for updated tokens.
🟠 NP Zone Time — Time at welfare/rest anchors (FL 0–4). Broken into Movement and Idle. Excluded from efficiency calculation. V2.3: FL0 (Lobby) = NP if dwell ≥10 min (genuine stop); <10 min treated as transit overhead. FL1–2 (Hoist/Welfare) = always full NP.
Idle time note: Only ~9 tokens currently carry Firmware v2 for idle reporting. As more tokens are updated, idle figures will become site-wide. Tokens without firmware show 0 idle.

How it works: Anchors detected in live data are listed below. Toggle any anchor to Non-Productive (welfare, hoist, dining, etc.) — those sessions will be excluded from the Efficiency % and shown in the orange bar on the Overview page. All other anchors are treated as Productive by default. As new zones are built out or re-purposed on site, just update the tagging here — changes persist in the browser and apply on the next data refresh.

🗓️ Classification Change Log

Audit trail of productive / non-productive anchor classification. Each session is scored using the rules in force on its own work-day, so changes apply forward only and historical performance never moves.

🔒 History is preserved
Work-days before a change keep their original classification; the new rules apply only from the change date forward (aligned to the 06:00 Dubai work-day boundary). To record a future change, a new era is appended — existing eras are never edited.
Non-Productive Anchors — current era

Classification logic (priority order): 1. Floor Config (primary) — Floors 1–4 are always classified as NP (ground truth for Verde building). This fires even if the API Tag says "productive". 2. API Tag field — used only for floors 5+ not in the floor config. If Tag = "non-productive" or "np", classified as NP. The Source column shows which rule classified each anchor. Floor Config should dominate for all welfare/dining/hoist areas.

⚙️ How avg wait is calculated: Hoist avg wait = arithmetic mean of genuine wait episodes only — sessions where dwell ≥ 60 s or the worker was detected by ≥ 2 distinct anchors in the same time window. Brief pass-throughs (<60 s, single anchor) are excluded as they reflect workers who walked past, not waited to board. Lift avg wait = mean of all dwell periods at lift anchors (lift has fewer false-positives so no filtering applied). Both averages are episode-level (each visit counts once), not worker-level. The Avg/Worker cards above show total dwell divided across unique workers. The zone-by-zone Session Average on the Overview page uses session durations from the main dataset and differs in scope.

🔬 Idle Time Diagnostics

Per-worker session detail — how idle time is calculated from raw anchor data

Time Accounting Model
Each anchor detection record has a start_time and end_time on a specific floor. Gaps between consecutive sessions are classified as follows:

🟢 Active — sum of anchor session durations. Worker confirmed on a construction floor.
🔵 Transit — gaps ≤ 30 min (threshold: 1800s). Worker likely between floors, queuing at lift, or briefly off-anchor.
⚪ Idle (alerts) — reserved for real TokenME idle-alert events. The token is designed to send an alert when dormant >15s (relayed by anchors every 60s). Currently 0 — pending API integration with TokenME.
🔴 Uncovered = max(0, 11h − active − transit). Includes gaps >30 min and any shift time with no anchor detection. Workers spending long periods in rest areas, lower floors, or zones without anchors will appear here — this is a coverage gap, not necessarily idle time.

Note: The 30-min transit threshold reflects observed lift-waiting times on site. Gaps >30 min are presumed to be in unanchored zones rather than stationary idle.
Workers Analysed
—

—

Performance Value

Site utilisation (active ÷ planned shift) against target & return on productive labour hours — Verde Building

—
Assumptions
Site Efficiency — Current
Productive Active ÷ Detected Span · V2.4.1
Target Utilisation
UAE construction RTLS benchmark
Gap to Close
percentage points to reach target
How efficiency is calculated (V2.4.2): Site Efficiency = Productive Active ÷ Detected Span, computed per worker then averaged. Ghost tokens (span ≥ 2h, zero productive movement) are automatically excluded from both numerator and denominator. Productive Active = anchor-confirmed productive dwell only (floors 5+ only; transit excluded). V2.3: FL0 (Lobby) = NP if worker dwell ≥10 min (genuine stop), transit overhead if <10 min. FL1 (Hoist), FL2/FL3 (Dining area) fully NP. Uncovered gaps classified as NP — included in Total NP Hours. Detected span = first → last anchor detection per worker. Workers input is auto-populated from live API data.
Return on Closing the Gap
Labour Cost (Daily)
—
total wage bill per day
Hours Recovered / Day
—
at target vs current
Value Gained / Day
—
recovered labour value
Value Gained / Year
—
based on working days
Value at Each Efficiency Milestone
Milestone Efficiency Active hrs / worker / day Daily Labour Value Annual Labour Value Annual Gain vs Current
Labour value = workers × active hrs/day × hourly rate × days. Gain = additional value vs current baseline. Hourly rate derived from daily wage ÷ shift hours.

Used to fetch live assigned token count via GET /api/Token. Shared Bearer token from above.
Gaps shorter than this are ignored (signal noise)
Gaps ≤ this = Transit (lift queuing / floor changes). Gaps > this = Uncovered (no anchor zone). Default: 1800s (30 min). Idle (alerts) bucket is reserved for real TokenME idle-alert events.
Hours to add to API timestamps to get Dubai local time. 4 = API returns UTC (default — confirmed by live data: bars at 03:00/04:00/05:00 UTC = 07:00/08:00/09:00 Dubai). Set to 0 only if the API is ever changed to return Dubai time directly.
Field Mapping
Tokens removed before all calculations. Currently 0 excluded — all tokens (including TokenMe engineer/test and L-F-T/L-W-T anchor tokens) are visible. Add IDs here to hide specific tokens.
—
Raw API Response (last fetch)
No data yet — trigger a refresh to populate
📊 Data Source Map — API vs Manual Fallback
Purpose: This view shows which data fields are sourced from the TokenME API vs manual Excel/CSV uploads. Use this to brief your engineering team on which API endpoints need to be built or enhanced. Status is live — refreshes each time you open this tab.
Loading data source status…
Data Field Current Source API Field / Endpoint Status Engineering Action Required
Worker Movement Sessions ✓ Live API /api/v2/tokens/TokensMovement
tid, floor_id, start_time, end_time, duration_seconds		 Live Working — no action needed
Worker Name / Formen ✓ Live API formen field in TokensMovement Live Returns anchor/device name, not worker name. Worker name requires profile lookup.
Worker Profiles (name, trade, division) ⚠ Excel/CSV Fallback /api/v2/tokens/WorkerProfiles
(endpoint not yet deployed)		 Fallback ACTION: Deploy worker profiles endpoint. Fields needed: tid → worker_name, trade, division, foreman_name, nationality
Token Assignment Register ⚠ Excel/CSV Fallback /api/v2/tokens/Assignment
(endpoint not yet deployed)		 Fallback ACTION: Deploy token assignment endpoint. Fields: tid, assigned_to, issue_date, status (active/returned)
Floor / Anchor Mapping ✓ Live API floor_id in TokensMovement; formen = anchor device name Live Working. Consider adding anchor metadata endpoint with floor number, zone type, install date.
Idle / Dormancy Alerts ⛔ Not Available /api/v2/tokens/IdleAlerts
(pending firmware upgrade)		 Blocked ACTION: Firmware upgrade required on tokens (coordinating with TokenME). Endpoint needs: tid, idle_start, idle_duration_seconds
SOS / Emergency Alerts ⚠ Simulated /api/v2/tokens/SOS
(endpoint to confirm)		 Partial ACTION: Confirm SOS endpoint. Current dashboard uses sample data. Need: tid, sos_time, floor_id, acknowledged_by
Gate Entry / Exit ✓ Live (derived) First/last session timestamp per token per day Live Derived from anchor data. A dedicated gate anchor at site entry would give cleaner data.
Non-Productive Zone Config ⚠ Manual Config Hardcoded in dashboard (lunch rooms, lifts, rest areas) Manual ACTION: Deploy zone type endpoint. Fields: anchor_id, zone_type (productive/welfare/transit), floor_id
Attendance / Headcount ✓ Live (derived) Counted from unique tids with sessions per day Live Working. Improve accuracy with a dedicated /Attendance endpoint that includes expected headcount per day.
📋 Engineering Handover Summary:
Live & Working: Worker movement sessions, floor/anchor mapping, gate entry/exit (derived), attendance (derived)
Needs API Endpoint: Worker profiles (name/trade/division), token assignment register, zone type configuration
Blocked on Firmware: Idle/dormancy alerts (TokenME firmware upgrade in progress)
Needs Confirmation: SOS/emergency alerts endpoint structure
System Settings
Connect to the API first, then open this tab to see field completeness diagnostics.

⚠ Not Detected interpretation: May reflect workers absent from site or coverage gaps (no anchor in work zone). Until entry anchors deploy at the main gate, this cannot be distinguished. Track over multiple shifts to separate absence from coverage gaps.
